creator: ac199@hwcn.org
text:
>        Because qmail likes the uid/gid to be compiled in, the numbers
>        are predicted beforehand - if the actual 'pw add' ends up using

We handle this by reserving uids.

>        Includes 'make replace' to disable and replace sendmail. Does not

cool.

>        currently include dot-forward (to support .forward files) or
>        fastforward (to support /etc/aliases) as I don't use them but
>        I'll add them if there's a demand.

As separate ports.


>        The port also fetches, patches and builds checkpassword, as
>        required for the pop3d (but not provided in qmail tarball)

Why?  Shouldn't that be a separate port?  Even Dan's var-qmail port
doesn't include that.


>        Uninstall leaves the users, groups and config directories and prints
>        a message to that effect. make install adds samples to inetd.conf
>        (commented out) and /etc/rc.d/qmail.sh (not executable). make replace
>        uncomments inetd.conf lines, makes qmail.sh executable and disables
>        sendmail in rc.conf - all seems to work ok.

Ports aren't allowed to touch /etc/.  It's fine if all touching is
done while in the "replace" target.  I would prefer that you submitted
a script instead of a "replace" target, however.  A script would allow
users who have installed with the qmail package to run it too.
Provide a nice script that does this, and I'll happily add you to the
"Additional Contributers" list, too.  :-)


>        this is my first port so any comments would be particularly helpful!

Yes: check the PR list at http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr-summary.cgi
next time before submitting a new port.


> X@unexec if [ -f /usr/sbin/sendmail.BAK ]; then if [ -r /usr/sbin/sendmail
> ]; then if diff -q /usr/sbin/sendmail $PKG_PREFIX/qmail/bin/sendmail;
> then rm /usr/ sbin/sendmail; mv /usr/sbin/sendmail.BAK /usr/sbin/sendmail;
> chmod 755 /usr/sbi n/sendmail; echo "replaced sendmail wrapper with
> original sendmail binary"; fi;
> fi; fi

Hmm...  There's more to returning sendmail to its original form than
just this single line, though...  It may perhaps be best to just tell
the user "Beware!  Beware!", or a reasonable paraphrase there-of.


> Xqmail/rc

Don't remove configuration files during pkg_delete.


> X@unexec /bin/rm /etc/rc.d/qmail.sh

A user may be pkg_deleting simply because they want to upgrade.  This
could erase some user-configured data.  Besides that, it should be in
/usr/local/etc/rc.d/.

> X@unexec echo "I haven't removed these groups: nofiles, qmail"
> X@unexec echo "or these users: alias, qmaild, qmaill, qmailp, qmailq, qmailr,
> X@unexec echo "alias/queue/control directories and rc file are left in $PKG_PR

Could be added to the "permanently-delete-qmail" script.  ``pw
userdel'' deletes users.


echo x - qmail/files/qmail.sh
sed 's/^X//' >qmail/files/qmail.sh << 'END-of-qmail/files/qmail.sh'
X#!/bin/sh

My preference is to just ``ln -s /var/qmail/rc /usr/local/etc/rc.d/qmail.sh''.


echo x - qmail/files/maildir
sed 's/^X//' >qmail/files/maildir << 'END-of-qmail/files/maildir'
X#!/bin/sh

What's this file do?


XALL_TARGET=    it dot-qmail.5 qmail-control.5 qmail-getpw.8 \
X               qmail-limits.7 qmail-newmrh.8 qmail-newu.8 \
X               qmail-pw2u.8 qmail-send.8 qmail-start.8 qmail-users.5

aka: "it man"  :-)


-- 
This .sig is not innovative, witty, or profund.
